steps.title: Spot the defining phrase
steps.content: The passage defines the features as "those that result from impromptu deviations of performers when fidelity to an established musical score is not mandated." The word impromptu is the key — it means spontaneous, done without advance planning. We need a word expressing this meaning.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean spontaneous or unplanned — the features arise from performers deviating from the score in the moment. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — inevitable: means certain to happen — implies the deviations are unavoidable, not that they are spontaneous or improvised. ✗
  • Choice B — inconspicuous: means not easily noticed — describes visibility, not the spontaneous nature of the deviations. ✗
  • Choice C — extemporaneous: means done without preparation, improvised on the spot — directly synonymous with impromptu deviations from a score. ✓
  • Choice D — meticulous: means extremely careful and precise — the opposite of improvised; meticulous performers would follow the score, not deviate from it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
